behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handler+signature-verifier+queue handoff: reject path must both set HTTP 401 and suppress enqueue side effect. MOCK-MISS applies because this is cross-module control flow, not a single pure function. Rejected higher level: no behavior text requires real network/OS/subprocess ('request...rejected with a 401' can be exercised in-process), so System is disqualified; no AI dependency, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Run the webhook route in-process with wired signature verification and queue adapter. Send a body with an invalid X-Signature-256. Assert HTTP 401 and assert queue write was not invoked/persisted.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is ordering across modules (HTTP handler and persistent queue). A real failure is missing/incorrect await or transaction boundary between enqueue and ack. Integration is the floor because behavior text explicitly requires cross-module order. Rejected higher level: no explicit need for real OS/network/subprocess; in-process app+real queue module (local DB/file-backed) is sufficient, so System is disqualified. No AI steps.
behaviors.test_description: Use the real handler and real persistent queue module in a local test setup. Instrument enqueue completion and response completion timestamps/events. Assert enqueue commit completes before HTTP 200 is observed.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Behavior tests decoupling between enqueue/ack path and downstream worker execution. The failure appears at module interaction (handler awaiting worker by mistake). Rejected higher level: behavior text only requires not waiting for downstream processing, which can be validated with in-process slow worker stub and route invocation; no mandatory real OS/network condition, so System is disqualified. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler + queue + downstream processor where processor intentionally blocks/sleeps. Send valid webhook and assert HTTP response returns before processor completion signal.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA applies: strict wall-clock SLA under concurrency depends on runtime scheduling, event loop contention, connection handling, and real HTTP serving behavior. In-process mocks can hide latency/regression sources. Rejected lower level: behavior text explicitly requires 'within 3 seconds wall-clock' under 'slow and concurrent' load, which disqualifies pure Integration timing with fakes.
behaviors.test_description: Start the actual service listener, generate concurrent prior events that trigger slow downstream work, then send a new valid webhook over real HTTP. Measure end-to-end response time and assert <=3s across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Duplicate suppression is a state handoff/persistence interaction (handler + dedupe check + queue store). Real failure often occurs across module boundaries (race/order/idempotency check location). Rejected higher level: behavior text does not require real network/OS/subprocess; local integration with persistent store is enough, so System is disqualified. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Send the same event ID twice through the route with valid signatures using wired dedupe + persistent queue components. Assert first request writes once, second does not create an additional queued record.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: This verifies contract propagation between queue adapter status and HTTP response mapping (status code + header). Failure boundary is module interaction, not single-function pure logic in isolation. Rejected higher level: behavior text says 'queue reports it is full' (reported condition can be simulated in-process), so real OS/network/subprocess is not required; System disqualified. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with queue module configured/test-double to emit a 'full' condition. Send valid webhook and assert HTTP 503 plus Retry-After header value/presence.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: This targets queue component behavior with real persistence/state across writes; minimum realistic boundary is queue module + local store wiring. MOCK-HIDE applies because a permissive fake queue would miss real capacity enforcement bugs. Rejected higher level: no explicit requirement for real network/subprocess/OS-specific primitives beyond local persistence, so System is disqualified. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize queue with a small configured capacity in a local persistent test store. Enqueue up to limit, then enqueue one more and assert rejection/error contract and unchanged stored count.
